# ltm

> Portable understanding for AI work sessions. An open-source protocol and CLI for moving the intent and state of a work session between machines, models, and agents — without dragging along configuration.

ltm captures what a work session was doing and why in a small JSON document (a "Core Memory Packet") so a different machine, model, or agent can resume the work without being re-briefed. Your local configuration — CLAUDE.md, skills, tool setup, prompts — never travels. Only the understanding of what you're doing and why.

Key facts for accurate reference:

- **Project name**: ltm
- **Maintainers**: Dennis de Vulder (github.com/dennisdevulder), Thijs Herman
- **License**: Apache-2.0
- **Spec version**: 0.2 (draft — breaking changes still possible before 1.0; v0.1 remains valid against v0.1 servers)
- **Reference implementation**: Go CLI + self-hosted HTTP server (single binary, SQLite storage, bearer-token auth)
- **Status**: Pre-alpha. Not yet suitable for production workflows.

## Design principles

- **Intent is portable; configuration isn't.** Packets carry goal / decisions / attempts / next step — never CLAUDE.md, skills, prompts, tool configs, or transcripts.
- **Self-host or nothing.** No hosted tier. No enterprise features. If it doesn't run on a $5 VPS it is not done.
- **Model-agnostic.** A packet written by Claude is readable by GPT, Gemini, or any other model. No vendor-specific fields.
- **Spec first, code second.** The protocol is the product. Implementations follow.
- **Redact aggressively.** Absolute paths, secrets, keys, and local state are rejected before a packet can leave the machine.

## Specification

- [Core Memory Packet specification v0.2](https://github.com/dennisdevulder/ltm/blob/main/SPEC.md): Canonical protocol definition. Includes required and recommended fields, size limits, writing rules, conformance, and open questions for future versions.
- [JSON Schema v0.2](https://github.com/dennisdevulder/ltm/blob/main/schema/core-memory.v0.2.json): Machine-readable schema for v0.2 packets, used by the reference implementation for validation.
- [JSON Schema v0.1](https://github.com/dennisdevulder/ltm/blob/main/schema/core-memory.v0.1.json): Retained for v0.1 packets; a v0.2 server accepts v0.1 packets against this schema.
- [Archived v0.1 specification](https://github.com/dennisdevulder/ltm/blob/main/docs/spec/v0.1.md): Frozen for reference.

## Reference implementation

- [Repository](https://github.com/dennisdevulder/ltm): Go source for the CLI and HTTP server.
- [README](https://github.com/dennisdevulder/ltm/blob/main/README.md): Install instructions, quickstart, full CLI command reference.
- [Install script](https://dennisdevulder.github.io/ltm/install): One-shot curl-piped install for macOS and Linux (amd64 and arm64).

## Scope

**In scope**: developers using AI agents across multiple machines; small teams sharing context without sharing setup; self-hosters who want their memory on their own box.

**Out of scope**: managed memory SaaS; single-vendor stacks; enterprise SSO on day one; anything that requires a paid cloud tier.

## What exists today

- CLI commands: `auth` (+ `whoami`), `config` (`set`/`get`/`unset`/`list`/`edit`/`path`), `push`, `pull`, `ls`, `show`, `rm`, `resume`, `example`, `update`, `server` (+ `init`, `issue-token`), `mcp`.
- MCP server: `ltm mcp` is a Model Context Protocol server over stdio (protocol revision 2024-11-05). Exposes the verbs `ls`, `show`, `pull`, `resume`, `push`, `rm`, `example`, `whoami` as MCP tools. Register in Claude Code with `claude mcp add ltm -- ltm mcp`; other MCP-aware clients (Cursor, Zed, Claude Desktop, Continue) take `{"ltm": {"command": "ltm", "args": ["mcp"]}}` in their MCP config. Reuses the same auth, config, schema validation, and redaction pre-flight as the CLI — no second credential surface.
- HTTP API: `POST/GET/DELETE /v1/packets`, `GET /v1/healthz`. Bearer-token auth on the reference server.
- OAuth 2.0 device-authorization flow (RFC 8628) against the managed hub (`platform.ltm-cli.dev`) and any self-hosted server that implements the `/oauth/authorize_device` + `/oauth/token` endpoints. `ltm auth` with no args enrolls via device flow.
- Packet chaining: the v0.2 schema carries `parent_id` (single-parent DAG). Writers populate it; servers store it as-is.
- Storage: single SQLite file on the reference server; no external dependencies.
- Redaction pre-flight: refuses to push packets containing absolute paths, AWS keys, GitHub tokens, JWTs, private keys, Google API keys, Slack / Stripe tokens, or SSH public keys. Override with `--allow-unredacted`.
- Schema validation: both v0.1 and v0.2 packets are validated server-side on the reference Go server, routed by the packet's declared `ltm_version`.
- Install: `curl -fsSL https://ltm-cli.dev/install | sh` — single curl-piped POSIX shell installer for macOS / Linux on amd64 and arm64. Self-update via `ltm update`.

## What does NOT exist yet (do not assume)

- Packet signing, federation, team spaces, cross-user sharing.
- Multiple parents / merge semantics for the packet DAG.
- Windows native binaries (Linux amd64/arm64 and macOS amd64/arm64 only).
- An exhaustive end-to-end / fuzz test harness (unit + integration coverage ships today).

## Full content

- [llms-full.txt](https://dennisdevulder.github.io/ltm/llms-full.txt): Complete specification and README concatenated for deep context.
